LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JEFFREY LEVIN MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. MOTION DENIED AS TO EXHIBITS A, B, AND C, PARAGRAPHS 2-6, 11-13, AND 56-59. MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 38-41, 47-48, 50-53, 62-66, 76, AND 80 REGARDING STATE FARM AND SETTLEMENT NEGOTIATIONS, AND PAGE 1, LINE 14-15, PAGE 32 PARAGRAPHS 2 AND 3; AND PAGE 33 PARAGRAPH 11. PLAINTIFF WILL AMEND THE COMPLAINT WITHIN TEN DAYS TO DELETE SPECIFIC AMOUNTS FOR DAMAGES, ATTORNEY'S FEES FOR BRINGING THIS TORT ACTION, REFERENCES TO STATE FARM AND SETTLEMENT NEGOTIATIONS, AND WILL NAME DEFENDANT AS AN INDIVIDUAL TO THE SUIT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CANNOT PLEAD A THIRD PARTY BAD FAITH CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S SETTLEMENT NEGOTIATIONS AND ACTIONS DO NOT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE A CLAIM. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND THE SIXTH, SEVENTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. CLAIM MAY NOT HAVE ACCRUED UNTIL FEBRUARY 11, 2009 WHEN PLAINTIFF BECAME AWARE OF THE EXTENT OF HER INJURIES. PLAINTIFF MAY STILL BE ABLE TO FILE A LATE CLAIM WITH THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF REBECCA MANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FAILS TO IDENTIFY EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, CLAIM, ISSUE OF DUTY OR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. THE SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS DOES NOT SEPARATELY SET THEM OUT, AND THE NOTICE OF MOTION DOES NOT REFERENCE THEM. (SEE: CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1350 ET SEQ. AND CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 437C). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISCUSS EACH ELEMENT FOR CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST LEVIN, AND FAILS TO SHOW HOW THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED SATISFIES EACH ELEMENT. C.C.P. 437C MAKES NO PROVISION FOR ADJUDICATION OF LIABILITY ALONE WHERE ISSUES OF THE CALCULATION OF DAMAGES REMAIN. DEPT. OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS V. U I VIDEO STORES, INC. (1997) 55 CAL. APP. 4TH 1084, 1097. THE COURT NEED NOT ADDRESS DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rebeca B. Mance, (859-484-2577) Plaintiff and moving party, in Propria Persona
Jeanette N. Littel, (415-267-3600) Philip M.Andersen & Associates, for defendant and opposing party: Jeffrey Levin, appearing telephonically

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF REBECCA MANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.
Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication fails to identify each cause of action, claim, issue of duty or affirmative defense. The separate statement of undisputed material facts does not separately set them out, and the notice of motion does not reference them. (See: California Rules of Court 3.1350 et seq. and Code of Civil Procedure 437c). Plaintiff fails to shift the burden of proof. Plaintiff does not discuss each element for causes of action against Levin, and fails to show how the evidence presented satisfies each element. C.C.P. 437c makes no provision for adjudication of liability alone where issues of the calculation of damages remain. Dept. of Industrial Relations v. U I Video Stores, Inc. (1997) 55 Cal. App. 4th 1084, 1097. The court need not address defendant's objections to evidence. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797 =(302/CWW)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JEFFREY LEVIN MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike denied in part and granted in part. Motion denied as to Exhibits A, B, and C, paragraphs 2-6, 11-13, and 56-59. Motion to strike granted as to paragraphs 38-41, 47-48, 50-53, 62-66, 76, and 80 regarding State Farm and settlement negotiations, and page 1, line 14-15, page 32 paragraphs 2 and 3; and page 33 paragraph 11. Plaintiff will amend the complaint within ten days to delete specific amounts for damages, attorney's fees for bringing this tort action, references to State Farm and settlement negotiations, and will name defendant as an individual to the suit. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Mark D. Lipton, Deputy City Atty 554-4218 for deft & moving party; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action for dangerous condition. Plaintiff failed to file a claim. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend the sixth, seventh, and eighth causes of action. Claim may not have accrued until February 11, 2009 when plaintiff became aware of the extent of her injuries. Plaintiff may still be able to file a late claim with the City and County of San Francisco. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Rebecca B. Mance, Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing and opposing party (925) 648-1776; Dara Tang, Esq. of Hayes Davis Bonino Ellingson McLay Scott LLP (650) 637-9100, appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot plead a third party bad faith claim against defendant. Defendant's settlement negotiations and actions do not support a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
